reviewSummary.overallDecision: Proceed to Knowledge Authoring for accepted file-system concepts; merge compatibility concept if an existing object is present; enrich journaling and snapshots before authoring.
qualityNotes: The manifest is suitable for discovery review and does not contain draft Knowledge Objects.
Most core file-system concepts have enough evidence for authoring.
Feature-level discoveries are valid but should not become shallow standalone objects without enrichment.
Speech-to-text errors must be normalized during authoring: XFAT→exFAT, TFS→NTFS, RFS/RIFFS→ReFS, terb→TB.

conceptReviews.title: File System
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Central concept; strong evidence and direct curriculum fit.

conceptReviews.title: Partition Formatting
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Strong procedural/prerequisite concept; keep separate from partition creation.

conceptReviews.title: Cross-Platform File-System Compatibility
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Avoid duplicate compatibility object; incorporate read/write support and removable-media examples.

notes: Normalize XFAT to exFAT in authoring notes.
Verify current read/write support wording for Linux and macOS before publishing.

conceptReviews.title: NTFS
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Core Windows file-system concept with features and contrasts.

conceptReviews.title: ReFS
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Valid Windows/server file-system concept; author with verification of current support limits.

notes: Verify exact ReFS desktop/server support during authoring.
Preserve source correction that RFS/RIFFS refers to ReFS.

conceptReviews.title: FAT32
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Exam-relevant limits and compatibility justify authoring.

conceptReviews.title: ext4
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Valid Linux/Android file-system concept; needs moderate enrichment.

conceptReviews.title: APFS
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Core Apple file-system concept with SSD, encryption, snapshots, and integrity features.

conceptReviews.title: File-System Journaling
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Important feature concept, but source coverage is brief and may fit under data integrity.

notes: Consider merging into storage.data-integrity if that concept already exists.
Do not over-expand beyond file-system reliability unless curriculum needs it.

conceptReviews.title: File-System Snapshots
conceptReviews.decisionReason: Valid feature-level discovery but only weakly mentioned; author only after enrichment.

notes: Keep tied to APFS unless a broader snapshot concept is intentionally introduced.

mergeActions.reason: The manifest explicitly identifies duplicate risk with a general file-system compatibility object.
Journaling may be better represented as a file-system reliability/data-integrity feature rather than a separate beginner-level object.

curriculumPlacementReview.reason: All reviewed concepts directly support the File Systems lesson and fit the existing file-systems module.

knowledgeGapReview.title: Partitioning vs formatting distinction
knowledgeGapReview.recommendedAction: Create or link prerequisite coverage distinguishing partition creation, formatting, and file-system selection.

knowledgeGapReview.title: Read support vs write support for file systems
knowledgeGapReview.recommendedAction: Add authoring guidance for read-only versus read-write compatibility across operating systems.

knowledgeGapReview.title: File-system feature vocabulary
knowledgeGapReview.recommendedAction: Create or link concise supporting concepts for compression, encryption, quotas, resiliency, integrity checking, snapshots, journaling, and fragmentation.

knowledgeGapReview.title: Current operating-system support needs verification
knowledgeGapReview.recommendedAction: Verify OS/version support before publishing exact compatibility claims.

rejectedMentionReview.title: Windows Server 2012
rejectedMentionReview.reason: Support/version example only; not a standalone concept for this lesson.

rejectedMentionReview.title: Check Disk
rejectedMentionReview.reason: Mentioned only for comparison; no procedure or troubleshooting depth in this source.

rejectedMentionReview.title: USB flash drive
rejectedMentionReview.reason: Example scenario for exFAT, not a taught hardware concept here.

rejectedMentionReview.title: macOS version 10.12.4
rejectedMentionReview.reason: Version detail belongs in APFS notes, not its own object.

rejectedMentionReview.title: Solid-state drives
rejectedMentionReview.reason: Relevant to APFS optimization but better linked as an existing storage-media prerequisite if available.

reviewNotes: Do not generate quiz questions, flashcards, or learner-facing explanations from this review artifact.
Use this review output as the gate between Transcript Intelligence discovery and Knowledge Authoring.
Accepted concepts still require canonical Knowledge Object authoring before becoming learner-facing content.
